question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a consensusTimestamp of 0 to prune(), causing the query 'delete from ContractResult where consensusTimestamp <= 0' to delete any ContractResult records with a zero or negative timestamp, potentially removing records that were inserted with default/uninitialized timestamps and had fee fields set?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authorization checks on prune() to repeatedly call it with incrementing consensusTimestamp values, progressively deleting all ContractResult fee records and making the gasConsumed/gasUsed/amount history unavailable for fee dispute resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with gasLimit set to Long.MAX_VALUE and gasUsed set to 1, causing the mirror node to report an extremely high gas limit with minimal gas usage, making the transaction appear to have been executed far below its fee cap and misleading fee estimation tools?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with both gasConsumed and gasUsed set to values that differ by more than the intrinsic gas cost (TX_BASE_COST=21000, TX_CREATE_EXTRA=32000), violating the design invariant established in calculateGasConsumed() and causing fee verification to fail silently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with contractId set to 0 (EntityId.EMPTY equivalent) while amount is non-zero, exploiting the lack of referential integrity enforcement to create a fee record attributed to a non-existent contract and corrupting fee-by-contract aggregation queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with bloom set to the 256-byte EMPTY_BLOOM value, exploiting the setBloom() normalization logic that converts EMPTY_BLOOM to an empty byte array, to cause the stored bloom filter to differ from what was actually emitted by the contract and affect fee-related log filtering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with functionResult containing a crafted protobuf-encoded ContractFunctionResult with manipulated gasUsed, exploiting the fact that functionResult is stored as raw bytes without re-validation, causing ContractResultMigration to later parse and apply the fraudulent gas values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with callResult set to a byte array that mimics a successful EVM return value while transactionResult indicates failure, causing fee refund logic to incorrectly classify the transaction and apply wrong fee adjustments?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user use findById() with a known consensusTimestamp to retrieve a ContractResult and then re-save it with modified gasConsumed/gasUsed/amount fields, exploiting the isNew()=true behavior to insert a duplicate record that shadows the original fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user call findAll() to enumerate all ContractResult records, extract payerAccountId and gasConsumed values, and use this information to identify high-value targets for fee manipulation attacks against specific accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: count()] Can an unprivileged external user use count() to determine the total number of ContractResult records and infer the volume of contract transactions, then time a prune() attack to coincide with low-activity periods to maximize the number of fee records deleted without detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with a consensusTimestamp in the far future, causing the record to be immune to prune() operations for an extended period while containing fraudulent fee data (inflated gasConsumed, manipulated amount) that persists in the system?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with transactionNonce set to Integer.MAX_VALUE, exploiting the int type (not Long) for transactionNonce to cause integer overflow when nonce-based fee deduplication arithmetic is performed, leading to incorrect fee deduplication?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Transactional annotation on RetentionRepository.prune() to cause a long-running transaction that holds a table lock on contract_result, blocking legitimate fee record insertions and causing a denial-of-service on fee recording?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with amount set to a negative value representing a negative HBAR fee, causing the mirror node's fee aggregation to undercount total fees charged and making it appear that the network collected less revenue than it actually did?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with createdContractIds containing the same contract ID multiple times, exploiting the List<Long> type without deduplication to cause fee records to be associated with the same child contract multiple times and inflate per-contract fee statistics?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with gasConsumed set to a value that is exactly TX_BASE_COST (21000) less than gasUsed, exploiting the calculateGasConsumed() formula to make the record appear as if it was processed by the legacy HAPI version path rather than the new throttling version, bypassing version-based fee validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() returns an int count of deleted rows without any minimum threshold check, to silently delete fee records in small batches that individually appear within normal retention behavior but cumulatively remove all fee history for a targeted contract?
